Ageusia.Description: complete loss of sense of taste

Dysgeusia.Description: disorder that distorts the sense of taste

Anosmia.Description: complete loss or absence of the sense of smell

Nasal congestion.Description: partial or complete blockage of your nasal passages

Odor identification.Description: ability to detect, recognize, and correctly name a specific smell

Disosmia.Description: any qualitative distortion, impairment, or abnormality in the sense of smell

Cacosmia.Description: disorder of the sense of smell where a person persistently perceives normal or even pleasant odors as foul, putrid, or vile

Hypoacusia.Description: a partial or total loss of hearing

Hyperacusia.Description: a hearing disorder where everyday, moderate noises sound uncomfortably or unbearably loud, and can sometimes cause physical pain

Painful hyperacusis.Description: a hearing disorder where ordinary, everyday sounds are perceived as abnormally loud and elicit physical ear pain or a burning, stabbing, or pressure-like sensation

Tinnitus.Description: hearing phantom noises,such as ringing, buzzing, hissing, or clicking,that do not originate from an outside source

Blinking lights.Description: sensation of seeing blinking, flashing, or flickering lights, or streaks of lightning in your field of vision

Visual acute.Description: sharpness and clarity of the vision

Photophobia.Description: extreme sensitivity to light that causes eye discomfort or pain

Dyplopia.Description: double vision, defined as simultaneously seeing two images of a single object

Blurry vision.Description: the eyesight has lost its sharpness, making objects appear hazy, dim, or out of focus

Light adaptation.Description: the eye's automatic process of adjusting its sensitivity when transitioning from a dark environment to a brightly lit one

Distance adaptation.Description: perceptual or physical adjustment in how spatial distance, proximity, or interpersonal space is sensed and managed

Eye Movements.Description: the voluntary and involuntary motions of the eyes that stabilize images on the retina, shift our line of sight, and allow us to track objects

Difficulty closing the eyes.Description: the nerve or muscles controlling the eyelids are weakened or damaged

Facial Assimetry.Description: the left and right sides of the face do not perfectly mirror each other

Nystagmus.Description: condition characterized by rapid, repetitive, and involuntary eye movements that cannot be controled

Ataxic gait.Description: an awkward, uncoordinated walking pattern characterized by a wide, unsteady stance, irregular foot placement, and staggering

Dizziness.Description: a range of sensations involving balance and spatial orientation

Vertigo.Description: a specific type of dizziness that creates the false sensation that you or your surroundings are spinning or moving when you are entirely still

Flaccid Dysarthria.Description: motor speech disorder caused by damage to the lower motor neurons in the peripheral nervous system

Paraparesis.Description: partial paralysis, weakness, or stiffness affecting both lower limbs

Decreased muscle strength in lower limbs.Description: a measurable loss of power or ability to generate force in the legs

Cramps.Description: a sudden, painful, and involuntary contraction or tightening of a muscle

Involuntary movement.Description: any body motion that happens suddenly and without conscious control

Spastic Dysarthria.Description: motor speech disorder caused by damage to the brain's upper motor neurons

Fatigue.Description: a state of extreme, persistent tiredness or exhaustion, physical, mental, or both that does not resolve with normal rest

Precise movements.Description: physical actions that are exact, carefully controlled, and executed with high accuracy and minimal wasted effort or deviation

Hypoesthesia.Description: numbness or a partial loss of sensation

Hyperesthesia.Description: an abnormal increase in sensory sensitivity, where your brain or nerves overreact to everyday stimuli

Dysesthesia.Description: an abnormal, unpleasant sensation produced by an ordinary, non-painful touch

Paresthesia.Description: abnormal skin sensations like tingling, pricking, burning, or numbness, commonly known as 'pins and needles'

Hypoalgesia.Description: a decreased or diminished sensitivity to painful stimuli

Hyperalgesia.Description: an abnormally increased sensitivity to pain

Alodinia.Description: experiencing pain from a stimulus that does not normally cause pain

Thermal sensibility.Description: nervous system's ability to perceive and distinguish temperature changes

Expressive aphasia.Description: a communication disorder where an individual knows what they want to say but struggles to speak or write the words

Receptive aphasia.Description: a neurological condition where a person has severe difficulty understanding spoken or written language

Coma.Description: a state of deep, prolonged unconsciousness where a person is alive but completely unresponsive

Insomnia.Description: a common sleep disorder characterized by persistent difficulty falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up too early despite having the opportunity to get adequate rest

Hypersomnia.Description: a sleep disorder characterized by excessive daytime sleepiness or the need to sleep for abnormally long periods

Dysuria.Description: pain, discomfort, or a burning sensation during urination

Fecal incontinence.Description: the accidental, involuntary passing of bowel contents—including solid stool, liquid stool, or gas—from the anus

Diarrhea.Description: a medical condition characterized by the frequent passage of loose, watery stools, typically occurring three or more times in a single day

Xerostomia.Description: the sensation of not having enough saliva to keep the mouth wet

Alacrima.Description: medical condition defined by the absence or severely reduced production of tears

Hyperhidrosis.Description: medical condition characterized by excessive and unpredictable sweating that goes far beyond what is necessary to regulate body temperature

Hypohidrosis.Description: medical condition characterized by a reduced, inadequate, or abnormally diminished ability to sweat

Postural hypotension.Description: a sudden, significant drop in blood pressure that happens immediately after you stand up from a sitting or lying position

Hypertensive episodes.Description: a sudden, severe surge in blood pressure

Cold extremities.Description: the hands, feet, fingers, or toes are significantly chillier than the rest of your body
